channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
item.description: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.content:encoded: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Trump administration delays rule aimed at improving disability access in schools
item.description: Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.
item.content:encoded:
Schools, colleges and other public institutions originally had until this week to make online content accessible to people with disabilities. Now, the Justice Department has delayed that deadline.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: Pittsburgh will close schools for NFL draft and switch to remote learning
item.description: The expected influx of fans later this week has led the school district to make a controversial decision: canceling in-person school.
item.content:encoded:
The expected influx of fans later this week has led the school district to make a controversial decision: canceling in-person school.

(Image credit: Perry Knotts)
item.dc:creator: Jillian Forstadt

item.title: School choice is booming in Iowa. Are students better off?
item.description: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.
item.content:encoded:
With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Filmmaker Ken Burns on the planned closure of his alma mater Hampshire College
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to filmmaker Ken Burns, an alumnus of Hampshire College, about the school's plan to permanently close at the end of the fall semester.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to filmmaker Ken Burns, an alumnus of Hampshire College, about the school's plan to permanently close at the end of the fall semester.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: More than a quarter of private colleges are at risk of closing, a new projection shows
item.description: As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.
item.content:encoded:
As one Vermont college finishes its last semester, an estimated 442 others may be in trouble.

(Image credit: Oliver Parini for The Hechinger Report)
item.dc:creator: Jon Marcus

item.title: Public university professors in Texas say a new law restricts their academic freedom
item.description: Public universities across Texas have instituted sweeping changes in recent months, from canceling gender studies programs to directing faculty to sign a pledge not to indoctrinate students.
item.content:encoded: Public universities across Texas have instituted sweeping changes in recent months, from canceling gender studies programs to directing faculty to sign a pledge not to indoctrinate students.
item.dc:creator: Camille Phillips

item.title: Colleges are trying to boost student voting. A Trump probe freezes data for that work
item.description: To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.
item.content:encoded:
To figure out how to boost student voting, colleges have relied on a study about campus voter registration and turnout rates. A Trump administration investigation has cut schools off from new data.

(Image credit: Angela Weiss)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: These blind students say their college blocked their education. A new rule could help
item.description: Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.
item.content:encoded:
Higher education is especially reliant on computers and phones, but accessibility for people with disabilities has often been forgotten. A new federal rule could change that.

(Image credit: Kristian Thacker for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo
